message:
test(fuzz): add symbol_envelope_decrypt fuzz target for fcp_protocol AEAD primitives

Adds a libfuzzer target that exercises the lower-level
fcp_protocol::symbol_envelope::{decrypt_symbol, derive_sender_subkey,
encrypt_symbol} APIs directly. Tracks bead flywheel_connectors-9seey.

Distinct from the existing raptorq_envelope_decrypt fuzz target (which fuzzes
the SymbolEnvelope::decrypt wrapper with envelope-aware struct + zone_key_id
matching gate). This target hits the raw AEAD primitives that the wrapper
composes:

- decrypt_symbol takes attacker-controlled ciphertext + auth_tag bytes
  directly, with a caller-supplied SymbolContext.
- derive_sender_subkey consumes a TailscaleNodeId whose bytes feed an HKDF
  Info field prefixed by a length-prefix — historically a fuzzer magnet.

Properties exercised:
1. Identity round-trip: encrypt_symbol + decrypt_symbol on the same key + ctx
   must round-trip the original plaintext.
2. Tampered ciphertext: any single-bit flip in ciphertext or auth_tag MUST
   surface as SymbolEnvelopeError::DecryptFailed (never silently accept,
   never panic).
3. Wrong subkey: feeding a different sender subkey (e.g. derived from a
   different sender_node_id) MUST fail authentication.
4. derive_sender_subkey: the documented `# Panics` clause says HKDF
   expansion never panics for the 32-byte output — the fuzz target
   asserts it across attacker-controlled length-prefixed sender_node_id
   bytes.

Two files:
- fuzz/Cargo.toml: registers fuzz_symbol_envelope_decrypt as a libfuzzer bin
  (test=false, doc=false, bench=false, matching every other entry).
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/symbol_envelope_decrypt.rs: the harness itself
  (~233 lines).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -312,6 +312,13 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_symbol_envelope_decrypt"
+path = "fuzz_targets/symbol_envelope_decrypt.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_sse_event_stream"
 path = "fuzz_targets/sse_event_stream.rs"
